1.text: Now I, Moroni, after finishing my summary of the account of the people of Jared, had not planned to write anything more. But I have not yet died, and I do not reveal myself to the Lamanites in case they would destroy me.
For behold, their wars among themselves are extremely fierce, and because of their hatred, they kill every Nephite who will not deny the Christ.
And I, Moroni, will not deny the Christ; so I wander wherever I can for the safety of my own life.

So I write a few more things, even though I had not planned to. I write a few more things that perhaps they may be of value to my brethren, the Lamanites, in some future day, according to the will of the Lord.

2.text: The words of Christ, which he spoke to his disciples, the twelve whom he had chosen, as he laid his hands upon them—

And he called them by name, saying: You shall call on the Father in my name, in mighty prayer; and after you have done this, you shall have the power to give the Holy Ghost to whoever you lay your hands on; and in my name you shall give it, for this is what my apostles do.
Now Christ spoke these words to them at the time of his first appearing; and the crowd did not hear it, but the disciples heard it. And on as many as they laid their hands, the Holy Ghost fell.

3.text: The way the disciples, who were called the elders of the church, ordained priests and teachers—
After they had prayed to the Father in the name of Christ, they laid their hands upon them and said:

In the name of Jesus Christ I ordain you to be a priest (or, if he be a teacher, I ordain you to be a teacher), to preach repentance and forgiveness of sins through Jesus Christ, by enduring in faith on his name to the end. Amen.
And this is how they ordained priests and teachers, according to the gifts and callings of God to people; and they ordained them by the power of the Holy Ghost, which was in them.

4.text: The way their elders and priests administered the flesh and blood of Christ to the church: they administered it according to the commandments of Christ, so we know the manner to be true. And the elder or priest administered it as follows—
And they knelt down with the church and prayed to the Father in the name of Christ, saying:

O God, the Eternal Father, we ask you in the name of your Son, Jesus Christ, to bless and sanctify this bread to the souls of all those who partake of it; that they may eat in remembrance of the body of your Son, and witness to you, O God, the Eternal Father, that they are willing to take upon them the name of your Son, and always remember him, and keep his commandments which he has given them, that they may always have his Spirit to be with them. Amen.

5.text: The way they administered the wine—Behold, they took the cup and said:

O God, the Eternal Father, we ask you, in the name of your Son, Jesus Christ, to bless and sanctify this wine to the souls of all those who drink of it, that they may do it in remembrance of the blood of your Son, which was shed for them; that they may witness to you, O God, the Eternal Father, that they do always remember him, that they may have his Spirit to be with them. Amen.

6.text: And now I speak about baptism. Behold, elders, priests, and teachers were baptized; and they were not baptized unless they showed by their actions that they were worthy of it.
They did not accept anyone for baptism unless that person came with a broken heart and a humble spirit, and testified to the church that they had truly repented of all their sins.
And no one was accepted for baptism unless they took upon them the name of Christ, with a determination to serve him to the end.

And after they had been baptized and were changed and cleansed by the power of the Holy Ghost, they were counted among the people of the church of Christ. And their names were recorded so they could be remembered and nourished by the good word of God, to keep them on the right path, to keep them always watchful in prayer, relying only on the merits of Christ, who was the author and the finisher of their faith.
And the church met together often to fast and to pray, and to talk with one another about the welfare of their souls.
And they met together often to partake of bread and wine in remembrance of the Lord Jesus.

And they were careful to make sure there was no wickedness among them. And whoever was found committing sin, if three witnesses of the church testified against them before the elders and they did not repent and confess, their names were removed, and they were no longer counted among the people of Christ.
But whenever they repented and sincerely sought forgiveness, with real intent, they were forgiven.

And their meetings were led by the church according to the guidance of the Spirit and by the power of the Holy Ghost; for wherever the power of the Holy Ghost led them—whether to preach, or to encourage, or to pray, or to plead, or to sing—that is what they did.

7.text: And now I, Moroni, write a few of the words of my father Mormon, which he spoke about faith, hope, and charity; for this is how he spoke to the people as he taught them in the synagogue they had built for worship.

And now I, Mormon, speak to you, my beloved brethren; and it is by the grace of God the Father, and our Lord Jesus Christ, and his holy will, because of the gift of his calling to me, that I am permitted to speak to you at this time.

Therefore, I want to speak to you who are members of the church, who are the peaceable followers of Christ, and who have gained enough hope that you can enter into the rest of the Lord, from this time forward until you rest with him in heaven.
And now my brethren, I can tell these things about you because of your peaceful way of living among other people.
For I remember the word of God which says: by their works you shall know them; for if their works are good, then they are good also.
For behold, God has said that an evil person cannot do that which is good; for if someone offers a gift or prays to God without real intent, it does them no good.
For behold, it is not counted as righteousness.
For behold, if an evil person gives a gift, they give it grudgingly; so it is counted the same as if they had kept the gift; therefore they are counted evil before God.
And likewise it is counted evil if a person prays without real intent of heart; yes, and it does them no good, for God does not accept such prayers.
Therefore, an evil person cannot do that which is good; neither will they give a good gift.

For behold, a bitter fountain cannot produce good water; neither can a good fountain produce bitter water. In the same way, a person serving the devil cannot follow Christ; and if they follow Christ, they cannot be a servant of the devil.

Therefore, all things which are good come from God; and that which is evil comes from the devil; for the devil is an enemy to God and fights against him continually, and invites and tempts people to sin and to do evil continually.

But behold, that which is of God invites and encourages people to do good continually; therefore, everything which invites and encourages people to do good, and to love God, and to serve him, is inspired of God.
Therefore, be careful, my beloved brethren, that you do not judge that which is evil to be of God, or that which is good and of God to be of the devil.

For behold, my brethren, you are given the ability to judge, so that you may know good from evil; and the way to judge is as plain as telling daylight from the dark night, so that you may know with a perfect knowledge.

For behold, the Spirit of Christ is given to every person, so that they may know good from evil. Therefore, I show you the way to judge: everything which invites people to do good and to believe in Christ is sent forth by the power and gift of Christ; and so you may know with a perfect knowledge that it is of God.

But whatever persuades people to do evil and to not believe in Christ, to deny him and not serve God, then you may know with a perfect knowledge that it is of the devil; for this is how the devil works—he persuades no one to do good, not even one person; neither do his angels; neither do those who give themselves over to him.

And now, my brethren, seeing that you know the light by which you may judge, which light is the light of Christ, be careful that you do not judge wrongfully; for with that same judgment which you judge, you shall also be judged.

Therefore, I plead with you, brethren, that you should search diligently in the light of Christ so that you may know good from evil; and if you will hold on to every good thing and not condemn it, you will certainly be a child of Christ.
And now, my brethren, how is it possible that you can hold on to every good thing?
And now I come to the topic of faith, which I said I would speak about; and I will tell you the way you may hold on to every good thing.

For behold, God, knowing all things, being from everlasting to everlasting, sent angels to minister to the children of men, to make known the coming of Christ; and in Christ every good thing would come.
And God also declared to prophets, by his own mouth, that Christ would come.

And behold, there were many different ways that he revealed good things to the children of men; and all things which are good come from Christ; otherwise people would be fallen, and no good thing could come to them.

Therefore, by the ministering of angels, and by every word which came from the mouth of God, people began to exercise faith in Christ; and so by faith, they held on to every good thing. And so it was until the coming of Christ.

And after he came, people were also saved by faith in his name; and by faith, they became the children of God. And as surely as Christ lives, he spoke these words to our fathers, saying: Whatever you shall ask the Father in my name, which is good, believing in faith that you shall receive it, behold, it shall be done for you.

Therefore, my beloved brethren, have miracles stopped because Christ has ascended into heaven and has sat down on the right hand of God, to claim from the Father the rights of mercy which he has for the children of men?

For he has fulfilled the demands of the law, and he claims all those who have faith in him; and those who have faith in him will hold on to every good thing. Therefore he pleads the cause of the children of men, and he lives eternally in the heavens.
And because he has done this, my beloved brethren, have miracles stopped? Behold I say to you, No; neither have angels stopped ministering to the children of men.
For behold, they are subject to him, to minister according to the word of his command, showing themselves to those of strong faith and a firm mind in every form of godliness.

And the purpose of their ministry is to call people to repentance, and to fulfill and do the work of the covenants of the Father, which he has made with the children of men, to prepare the way among the children of men by declaring the word of Christ to the chosen servants of the Lord, so that they may bear testimony of him.

And by doing this, the Lord God prepares the way so that the rest of the people may have faith in Christ, so that the Holy Ghost may have a place in their hearts, according to its power; and in this way the Father brings about the covenants which he has made with the children of men.
And Christ has said: If you will have faith in me, you shall have power to do whatever is right and necessary in me.
And he has said: Repent, all you ends of the earth, and come to me, and be baptized in my name, and have faith in me, that you may be saved.

And now, my beloved brethren, if these things which I have spoken to you are true, and God will show you with power and great glory at the last day that they are true—if they are true, has the day of miracles ended?

Or have angels stopped appearing to the children of men? Or has God withheld the power of the Holy Ghost from them? Or will he, so long as time shall last, or the earth shall stand, or there shall be one person on the face of the earth to be saved?

Behold I say to you, No; for it is by faith that miracles happen; and it is by faith that angels appear and minister to people. Therefore, if these things have stopped, then woe to the children of men, for it is because of unbelief, and all is in vain.

For no one can be saved, according to the words of Christ, unless they have faith in his name. Therefore, if these things have stopped, then faith has stopped also; and terrible is the state of mankind, for they would be as if no redemption had been made.

But behold, my beloved brethren, I believe better things about you, for I believe that you have faith in Christ because of your meekness; for if you do not have faith in him, then you are not fit to be counted among the people of his church.
And again, my beloved brethren, I want to speak to you about hope. How can you gain faith unless you first have hope?

And what is it that you should hope for? Behold I say to you that you should have hope through the atonement of Christ and the power of his resurrection, to be raised to eternal life, and this because of your faith in him according to the promise.
Therefore, if a person has faith, they must also have hope; for without faith there cannot be any hope.
And again, behold I say to you that a person cannot have faith and hope unless they are meek and humble in heart.

If not, their faith and hope are worthless, for no one is acceptable before God except the meek and humble in heart; and if a person is meek and humble in heart, and confesses by the power of the Holy Ghost that Jesus is the Christ, they must have charity; for if they do not have charity, they are nothing; therefore they must have charity.

And charity is patient and is kind; it does not envy and is not proud; it does not seek its own way, is not easily angered, thinks no evil, and does not rejoice in wickedness but rejoices in the truth. It bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, endures all things.
Therefore, my beloved brethren, if you do not have charity, you are nothing, for charity never fails. Therefore, hold on to charity, which is the greatest of all, for all things must fail—
But charity is the pure love of Christ, and it endures forever; and whoever possesses it at the last day, it shall be well with them.

Therefore, my beloved brethren, pray to the Father with all the energy of heart, that you may be filled with this love, which he has given to all who are true followers of his Son, Jesus Christ; that you may become the children of God; that when he shall appear we shall be like him, for we shall see him as he is; that we may have this hope; that we may be purified even as he is pure. Amen.

8.text: A letter from my father Mormon, written to me, Moroni; and it was written to me soon after I was called to the ministry. And this is what he wrote to me:
My beloved son, Moroni, I rejoice greatly that your Lord Jesus Christ has been mindful of you and has called you to his ministry and to his holy work.

I always remember you in my prayers, continually praying to God the Father in the name of his Holy Child, Jesus, that he, through his infinite goodness and grace, will keep you through your endurance of faith on his name to the end.
And now, my son, I write to you about something that grieves me deeply; for it grieves me that there are disputes rising among you.
For, if I have learned correctly, there have been disputes among you about the baptism of your little children.
And now, my son, I want you to work hard to have this serious error removed from among you; for this is why I have written this letter.
For as soon as I learned these things about you, I asked the Lord about it. And the word of the Lord came to me by the power of the Holy Ghost, saying:

Listen to the words of Christ, your Redeemer, your Lord and your God. Behold, I came into the world not to call the righteous but sinners to repentance; the healthy do not need a doctor, only those who are sick. Therefore, little children are whole, for they are not capable of committing sin. Therefore the curse of Adam is taken from them in me, so that it has no power over them; and the law of circumcision is done away in me.
And this is how the Holy Ghost revealed the word of God to me. Therefore, my beloved son, I know that it is a solemn mockery before God to baptize little children.

Behold I say to you that this is what you should teach—repentance and baptism for those who are accountable and capable of committing sin. Yes, teach parents that they must repent and be baptized and humble themselves like their little children, and they shall all be saved along with their little children.
And their little children need no repentance, neither baptism. Behold, baptism leads to repentance and to fulfilling the commandments for the forgiveness of sins.

But little children are alive in Christ, even from the foundation of the world; if this is not so, then God is a partial God, a changeable God, and one who shows favoritism; for how many little children have died without baptism!
Therefore, if little children could not be saved without baptism, they would have gone to an endless hell.

Behold I say to you that whoever thinks that little children need baptism is in the bitterness of sin and in the chains of wickedness; for that person has neither faith, hope, nor charity. Therefore, if they should die while thinking this, they must go down to hell.
For it is terrible wickedness to suppose that God saves one child because of baptism while the other must perish because they had no baptism.

Woe to those who twist the ways of the Lord like this, for they shall perish unless they repent. Behold, I speak with boldness, having authority from God; and I do not fear what any person can do, for perfect love drives out all fear.
And I am filled with charity, which is everlasting love; therefore, all children are alike to me. I love little children with a perfect love, and they are all alike and share in salvation.
For I know that God is not a partial God, neither a changeable being; but he is unchangeable from all eternity to all eternity.
Little children cannot repent; therefore, it is terrible wickedness to deny the pure mercies of God to them, for they are all alive in him because of his mercy.
And whoever says that little children need baptism denies the mercies of Christ and treats the atonement and the power of his redemption as worthless.

Woe to such people, for they are in danger of death, hell, and an endless torment. I say it boldly; God has commanded me. Listen to these words and pay attention, or they will stand against you at the judgment seat of Christ.

For behold, all little children are alive in Christ, and also all those who are without the law. For the power of redemption comes upon all who have no law; therefore, those who are not condemned, or those who are under no condemnation, cannot repent; and for them, baptism accomplishes nothing—
But it is mockery before God, denying the mercies of Christ and the power of his Holy Spirit, and putting trust in dead works.
Behold, my son, this should not be; for repentance is for those who are under condemnation and under the curse of a broken law.
And the first fruit of repentance is baptism; and baptism comes by faith to fulfill the commandments; and fulfilling the commandments brings forgiveness of sins.

And the forgiveness of sins brings meekness and humility of heart; and because of meekness and humility of heart comes the visitation of the Holy Ghost, which Comforter fills a person with hope and perfect love, which love endures through diligent prayer until the end shall come, when all the saints shall live with God.

Behold, my son, I will write to you again if I do not soon go out to fight against the Lamanites. Behold, the pride of this nation, the people of the Nephites, has led to their destruction unless they repent.

Pray for them, my son, that repentance may come to them. But behold, I fear that the Spirit has stopped working with them; and in this part of the land they are also trying to destroy all power and authority that comes from God; and they are denying the Holy Ghost.
And after rejecting such great knowledge, my son, they must soon perish, fulfilling the prophecies which were spoken by the prophets, as well as the words of our Savior himself.
Farewell, my son, until I write to you again, or meet you again. Amen.

9.text: My beloved son, I write to you again so you may know that I am still alive; but I write about things that are deeply troubling.

For behold, I have had a terrible battle with the Lamanites, in which we did not win; and Archeantus has fallen by the sword, and also Luram and Emron; yes, and we have lost a great number of our finest men.
And now behold, my son, I fear that the Lamanites will destroy this people; for they do not repent, and Satan stirs them up continually to be angry with one another.

Behold, I work with them continually; and when I speak the word of God with sharpness, they tremble and become angry at me; and when I do not use sharpness, they harden their hearts against it. Therefore, I fear that the Spirit of the Lord has stopped working with them.
For they are so full of anger that it seems to me they have no fear of death; and they have lost their love for one another; and they thirst after blood and revenge continually.

And now, my beloved son, despite their hardness, let us keep working; for if we stop working, we would be brought under condemnation; for we have a work to do while in this body of clay, so that we may conquer the enemy of all righteousness and rest our souls in the kingdom of God.

And now I write something about the sufferings of this people. According to the information I received from Amoron, behold, the Lamanites have many prisoners, which they took from the tower of Sherrizah; and there were men, women, and children.

And the husbands and fathers of those women and children they have killed; and they feed the women the flesh of their husbands, and the children the flesh of their fathers; and they give them only a little water.

And despite this great horror from the Lamanites, it does not surpass the crimes of our own people in Moriantum. For behold, they have taken many of the daughters of the Lamanites as prisoners; and after taking from them that which was most dear and precious above all things, which is their chastity and virtue—

And after they had done this, they murdered them in the cruelest way, torturing their bodies even to death; and after doing this, they devoured their flesh like wild animals, because of the hardness of their hearts; and they did it as a sign of bravery.
O my beloved son, how can a people like this, who are without civilization—
(And only a few years have passed since they were a civilized and a delightful people)
But O my son, how can a people like this, whose delight is in so much horror—
How can we expect that God will hold back his judgment against us?
Behold, my heart cries out: Woe to this people! Come out in judgment, O God, and hide their sins, wickedness, and abominations from before your face!

And again, my son, there are many widows and their daughters who remain in Sherrizah; and the supplies that the Lamanites did not carry away, the army of Zenephi has taken, and left them to wander wherever they can for food; and many old women faint along the way and die.
And the army which is with me is weak; and the armies of the Lamanites are between Sherrizah and me; and as many as have fled to the army of Aaron have fallen victims to their awful brutality.
O the wickedness of my people! They are without order and without mercy. Behold, I am only a man, and I have only the strength of a man, and I can no longer enforce my commands.

And they have become strong in their wickedness; and they are equally brutal, sparing no one, neither old nor young; and they delight in everything except that which is good; and the suffering of our women and our children throughout this land is beyond description; yes, words cannot tell it, neither can it be written.

And now, my son, I will say no more about this horrible scene. Behold, you know the wickedness of this people; you know that they are without principles and past feeling; and their wickedness is worse than the Lamanites'.
Behold, my son, I cannot pray for them to God, lest he should strike me down.

But behold, my son, I pray for you to God, and I trust in Christ that you will be saved; and I pray to God that he will spare your life, so you may witness the return of his people to him, or their complete destruction; for I know that they must perish unless they repent and return to him.
And if they perish, it will be like the Jaredites, because of the stubbornness of their hearts, seeking for blood and revenge.

And if they do perish, we know that many of our brethren have gone over to the Lamanites, and many more will also go over to them. Therefore, write down a few things, if you are spared and I die without seeing you; but I hope that I may see you soon, for I have sacred records that I would like to give to you.

My son, be faithful in Christ; and may the things which I have written not grieve you or weigh you down to death; but may Christ lift you up, and may his sufferings and death, and the showing of his body to our fathers, and his mercy and patience, and the hope of his glory and of eternal life, rest in your mind forever.

And may the grace of God the Father, whose throne is high in the heavens, and our Lord Jesus Christ, who sits on the right hand of his power until all things shall become subject to him, be and remain with you forever. Amen.

10.text: Now I, Moroni, write what seems good to me; and I write to my brethren, the Lamanites; and I want them to know that more than four hundred and twenty years have passed since the sign was given of the coming of Christ.
And I seal up these records, after I have spoken a few words of encouragement to you.

Behold, I would encourage you that when you read these things, if it is the wisdom of God that you should read them, that you would remember how merciful the Lord has been to the children of men, from the creation of Adam even down until the time you receive these things, and ponder it in your hearts.

And when you receive these things, I would encourage you to ask God, the Eternal Father, in the name of Christ, if these things are not true; and if you ask with a sincere heart, with real intent, having faith in Christ, he will reveal the truth of it to you, by the power of the Holy Ghost.
And by the power of the Holy Ghost you may know the truth of all things.
And whatever is good is just and true; therefore, nothing that is good denies the Christ, but acknowledges that he is.

And you may know that he is, by the power of the Holy Ghost; therefore I would encourage you not to deny the power of God; for he works by power, according to the faith of the children of men, the same today and tomorrow and forever.

And again, I urge you, my brethren, not to deny the gifts of God, for they are many; and they come from the same God. And there are different ways that these gifts are given; but it is the same God who works all in all; and they are given through the manifestations of the Spirit of God to people, to benefit them.
For behold, to one person is given by the Spirit of God the ability to teach the word of wisdom;
And to another, the ability to teach the word of knowledge by the same Spirit;
And to another, exceedingly great faith; and to another, the gifts of healing by the same Spirit;
And again, to another, the ability to work mighty miracles;
And again, to another, the ability to prophesy about all things;
And again, to another, the seeing of angels and ministering spirits;
And again, to another, all kinds of tongues;
And again, to another, the interpretation of languages and of various kinds of tongues.
And all these gifts come by the Spirit of Christ; and they come to every person individually, according to his will.
And I would encourage you, my beloved brethren, to remember that every good gift comes from Christ.

And I would encourage you, my beloved brethren, to remember that he is the same yesterday, today, and forever, and that all these gifts of which I have spoken, which are spiritual, will never be taken away as long as the world shall stand, except because of the unbelief of the children of men.
Therefore, there must be faith; and if there must be faith, there must also be hope; and if there must be hope, there must also be charity.
And unless you have charity, you cannot be saved in the kingdom of God; neither can you be saved in the kingdom of God if you do not have faith; neither can you if you have no hope.
And if you have no hope, you will be in despair; and despair comes because of sin.
And Christ truly said to our fathers: If you have faith, you can do all things which are right and necessary for me.
And now I speak to all the ends of the earth—that if the day comes when the power and gifts of God are taken away from among you, it will be because of unbelief.

And woe to the children of men if this happens; for there will be no one who does good among you, not even one. For if there is one among you who does good, that person will work by the power and gifts of God.
And woe to those who reject these things and die, for they die in their sins and cannot be saved in the kingdom of God; and I say this according to the words of Christ; and I do not lie.

And I urge you to remember these things; for the time quickly comes when you will know that I do not lie, for you will see me at the judgment bar of God; and the Lord God will say to you: Did I not declare my words to you, which were written by this man, like one crying from the dead, yes, even like one speaking out of the dust?
I declare these things to fulfill the prophecies. And behold, they shall come forth from the mouth of the everlasting God; and his word shall go forth from generation to generation.
And God shall show you that what I have written is true.
And again I would encourage you to come to Christ, and hold on to every good gift, and do not touch the evil gift nor the unclean thing.

And awake and arise from the dust, O Jerusalem; yes, and put on your beautiful garments, O daughter of Zion; and strengthen your stakes and enlarge your borders forever, so that you may no more be overcome, so that the covenants of the Eternal Father which he has made with you, O house of Israel, may be fulfilled.

Yes, come to Christ, and be perfected in him, and deny yourselves of all ungodliness; and if you deny yourselves of all ungodliness and love God with all your might, mind, and strength, then his grace is sufficient for you, that by his grace you may be perfect in Christ; and if by the grace of God you are perfect in Christ, you cannot deny the power of God.

And again, if by the grace of God you are perfect in Christ, and do not deny his power, then you are sanctified in Christ by the grace of God, through the shedding of the blood of Christ, which is in the covenant of the Father for the forgiveness of your sins, so that you become holy, without spot.

And now I say farewell to all. I soon go to rest in the paradise of God, until my spirit and body shall reunite again, and I am brought forth triumphant through the air, to meet you before the pleasing bar of the great Jehovah, the Eternal Judge of both the living and the dead. Amen.
